normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU1_start_lt_stop (intervals : Intervals) : Prop :=
  ∀ i ∈ intervals, i.start < i.stop
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Input precondition: every interval satisfies start < stop.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_feasible_witness (intervals : Intervals) :
  ∃ idxs : List Nat,
    Feasible intervals idxs ∧
      idxs.length = intervalScheduling intervals
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned count is witnessed by some subset of the input that is pairwise non-overlapping.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_optimality (intervals : Intervals) :
  ∀ idxs : List Nat,
    Feasible intervals idxs → idxs.length ≤ intervalScheduling intervals
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No pairwise non-overlapping subset of the input is strictly larger than the returned count.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_endpoint_nonoverlap (a b : Interval) :
  a.stop = b.start → NonOverlap a b
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Two intervals that share only an endpoint are non-overlapping (closed-open convention).
Empty input: the result is 0 when the input is empty.
